Title: CharUnicodeInfo
Description: This class implements a set of methods for retrieving character type information. Character type information is independent of culture and region.
Body: This class implements a set of methods for retrieving character type information. Character type information is independent of culture and region.

Title: BigNumber
Description: Represents an arbitrary-precision decimal number using a significand and exponent.
Body:
Represents an arbitrary-precision decimal number using a significand and exponent.
Internally represented as: value = significand × 10^exponent where significand is a [`BigInteger`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.numerics.biginteger) and exponent is a [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32). This type provides equivalent functionality to [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal) but with arbitrary precision, similar to how [`BigInteger`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.numerics.biginteger) extends integer arithmetic beyond fixed sizes.
Significand: Gets the significand of the number.
Exponent: Gets the exponent (power of 10) of the number.
Zero: Gets a value representing zero.
One: Gets a value representing one.
MinusOne: Gets a value representing minus one.
Radix: Gets the radix (base) of the number system.
Normalize: Returns a normalized copy of this number with trailing zeros removed from the significand.
IsInteger: Determines whether this instance represents an integer value.
Equals: Determines whether the specified [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) is equal to this instance.
Equals: Determines whether the specified object is equal to this instance.
GetHashCode: Returns a hash code for this instance.
CompareTo: Compares this instance with another [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value.
CompareTo: Compares this instance with a specified object.
ToString: Returns the string representation of this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value.
ToString: Formats this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value using the specified format string and format provider.
TryGetMinimumFormatBufferLength: Gets the minimum format buffer length.
TryFormat: Tries to format this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value into the provided character span.
TryFormat: Tries to format this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value into the provided UTF-8 byte span.
TryFormat: Tries to format this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value into the provided character span using default formatting.
TryFormat: Tries to format this [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value into the provided UTF-8 byte span using default formatting.
Parse: Parses a string into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
Parse: Parses a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a string into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a string into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse a span of characters into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
Parse: Parses UTF-8 bytes into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
TryParse: Attempts to parse UTF-8 bytes into a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html).
Divide: Divides one [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) by another with specified precision.
Abs: Returns the absolute value.
Sign: Returns the sign of the number.
Pow: Raises a BigNumber to an integer power.
Sqrt: Computes the square root of a BigNumber using Newton's method.
Round: Rounds a value to a specified number of decimal places.
Floor: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to the specified number.
Ceiling: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to the specified number.
Truncate: Truncates a value to an integer by removing the fractional part.
TryFormatOptimized: Tries to format this instance into the provided UTF-16 span with zero allocations.
TryFormatUtf8Optimized: Tries to format this instance into the provided UTF-8 span with zero allocations.
TryParseJsonUtf8: Tries to parse a BigNumber from UTF-8 bytes in JSON format with zero allocations.

Title: ArrayEnumerator<TItem>

Body:

GetEnumerator: Returns an enumerator that iterates through the JSON array.
Dispose: Releases resources used by the enumerator.
Reset: Sets the enumerator to its initial position, which is before the first element in the collection.
MoveNext: Advances the enumerator to the next element of the collection.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>
Description: A mutable JSON document builder that provides functionality to construct and modify JSON documents.
Body:
A mutable JSON document builder that provides functionality to construct and modify JSON documents.
RootElement: Gets the root element of the JSON document.
WriteTo: Write the document into the provided writer as a JSON value.
CreateSnapshot: Creates a snapshot of this builder's current state. The snapshot can be used to cheaply restore the builder to this state via [`Restore`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.restore.html) without re-traversing the source document.
Restore: Restores this builder to the state captured in `snapshot`. The existing backing buffers are reused (they can only grow, never shrink), so this is a pure memcpy with no allocations.
Parse: Parses UTF-8 encoded JSON directly into a mutable document builder, avoiding the intermediate [`ParsedJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-parsedjsondocument-t.html) allocation and tree walk.
Parse: Parses text representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder.
Parse: Parses a string representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder.
Parse: Parses a [`Stream`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.io.stream) of UTF-8 encoded data representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder. The Stream will be read to completion.
ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader directly into a mutable document builder.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>.RootElement
Description: Gets the root element of the JSON document.
Body: Gets the root element of the JSON document.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>.WriteTo
Description: Write the document into the provided writer as a JSON value.
Body: Write the document into the provided writer as a JSON value.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>.CreateSnapshot
Description: Creates a snapshot of this builder's current state. The snapshot can be used to cheaply restore the builder to this state via [`Restore`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.restore.html) without re-traversing the source document.
Body: Creates a snapshot of this builder's current state. The snapshot can be used to cheaply restore the builder to this state via [`Restore`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.restore.html) without re-traversing the source document.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>.Restore
Description: Restores this builder to the state captured in `snapshot`. The existing backing buffers are reused (they can only grow, never shrink), so this is a pure memcpy with no allocations.
Body: Restores this builder to the state captured in `snapshot`. The existing backing buffers are reused (they can only grow, never shrink), so this is a pure memcpy with no allocations.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilder<T>.Parse
Description: Parses UTF-8 encoded JSON directly into a mutable document builder, avoiding the intermediate [`ParsedJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-parsedjsondocument-t.html) allocation and tree walk.
Body: Parses UTF-8 encoded JSON directly into a mutable document builder, avoiding the intermediate [`ParsedJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-parsedjsondocument-t.html) allocation and tree walk. Parses text representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder. Parses a string representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder. Parses a [`Stream`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.io.stream) of UTF-8 encoded data representing a single JSON value directly into a mutable document builder. The Stream will be read to completion.

Description: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader directly into a mutable document builder.
Body: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader directly into a mutable document builder.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilderSnapshot<T>
Description: A snapshot of a [`JsonDocumentBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.html)'s state that can be used to cheaply restore a builder to its initial state via [`Restore`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.restore.html), avoiding the cost of re-traversing the source document.
Body:
A snapshot of a [`JsonDocumentBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.html)'s state that can be used to cheaply restore a builder to its initial state via [`Restore`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsondocumentbuilder-t.restore.html), avoiding the cost of re-traversing the source document.
Dispose: Releases the rented buffers held by this snapshot.

Title: JsonDocumentBuilderSnapshot<T>.Dispose
Description: Releases the rented buffers held by this snapshot.
Body: Releases the rented buffers held by this snapshot.

Title: JsonDocumentOptions
Description: Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when parsing JSON to create a `JsonDocument`.
Body:
Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when parsing JSON to create a `JsonDocument`.
AllowTrailingCommas: Defines whether an extra comma at the end of a list of JSON values in an object or array is allowed (and ignored) within the JSON payload being read.
CommentHandling: Defines how the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) should handle comments when reading through the JSON.
MaxDepth: Gets or sets the maximum depth allowed when reading JSON, with the default (i.e. 0) indicating a max depth of 64.

Title: JsonDocumentOptions.AllowTrailingCommas
Description: Defines whether an extra comma at the end of a list of JSON values in an object or array is allowed (and ignored) within the JSON payload being read.
Body: Defines whether an extra comma at the end of a list of JSON values in an object or array is allowed (and ignored) within the JSON payload being read.

ToString: Converts the value of this instance to a [`String`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.string).

Title: JsonException
Description: Represents errors that occur during JSON parsing, reading, or writing operations. This exception is thrown when invalid JSON text is encountered, when the defined maximum depth is exceeded, or when the JSON text is not compatible with the type of a property on an object.
Body:
Represents errors that occur during JSON parsing, reading, or writing operations. This exception is thrown when invalid JSON text is encountered, when the defined maximum depth is exceeded, or when the JSON text is not compatible with the type of a property on an object.
BytePositionInLine: Gets the number of bytes read within the current line before the exception (starting at 0).
LineNumber: Gets the number of lines read so far before the exception (starting at 0).
Message: Gets a message that describes the current exception.
Path: Gets the path within the JSON where the exception was encountered.

Title: JsonReaderOptions
Description: Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when reading JSON.
Body:
Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when reading JSON.
AllowMultipleValues: Defines whether the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) should tolerate zero or more top-level JSON values that are whitespace separated.
AllowTrailingCommas: Defines whether an extra comma at the end of a list of JSON values in an object or array is allowed (and ignored) within the JSON payload being read.
CommentHandling: Defines how the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) should handle comments when reading through the JSON.
MaxDepth: Gets or sets the maximum depth allowed when reading JSON, with the default (i.e. 0) indicating a max depth of 64.

Body: Defines whether an extra comma at the end of a list of JSON values in an object or array is allowed (and ignored) within the JSON payload being read.

Body: Gets or sets the maximum depth allowed when reading JSON, with the default (i.e. 0) indicating a max depth of 64.

Title: JsonReaderState
Description: Defines an opaque type that holds and saves all the relevant state information which must be provided to the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) to continue reading after processing incomplete data. This type is required to support reentrancy when reading incomplete data, and to continue reading once more data is available. Unlike the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html), which is a ref struct, this type can survive across async/await boundaries and hence this type is required to provide support for reading in more data asynchronously before continuing with a new instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html).
Body:
Defines an opaque type that holds and saves all the relevant state information which must be provided to the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) to continue reading after processing incomplete data. This type is required to support reentrancy when reading incomplete data, and to continue reading once more data is available. Unlike the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html), which is a ref struct, this type can survive across async/await boundaries and hence this type is required to provide support for reading in more data asynchronously before continuing with a new instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html).
Options: Gets the custom behavior when reading JSON using the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) that may deviate from strict adherence to the JSON specification, which is the default behavior.

Description: Extracts JSON Schema annotations from a [`JsonSchemaResultsCollector`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaresultscollector.html) that has been used in [`Verbose`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaresultslevel.verbose.html) mode.
Body:
Extracts JSON Schema annotations from a [`JsonSchemaResultsCollector`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaresultscollector.html) that has been used in [`Verbose`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaresultslevel.verbose.html) mode.
Annotations in JSON Schema are produced by keywords that do not perform validation but instead attach metadata to the instance. When a [`JsonSchemaResultsCollector`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaresultscollector.html) is used in verbose mode, annotation-producing keywords emit results via `IgnoredKeyword`. This class filters those results and extracts them as structured annotation values. Each annotation is identified by: - Instance location: The JSON Pointer into the instance being validated (e.g., "", "/foo", "/items/0"). - Keyword: The annotation keyword name (e.g., "title", "description", "default"). - Schema location: The absolute JSON Pointer into the schema where the keyword appears (e.g., "#", "#/$defs/foo"). - Value: The raw JSON value of the annotation.
EnumerateAnnotations: Enumerates annotations from the collector as an [`AnnotationEnumerator`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaannotationproducer-annotationenumerator.html).
WriteAnnotationsTo: Writes all annotations from the collector as a JSON object to the given writer.
CollectAnnotations: Collects all annotations from the collector into a dictionary keyed by (instanceLocation, keyword), with values being a dictionary of schemaLocation → annotationValue.

Title: JsonSchemaAnnotationProducer.EnumerateAnnotations
Description: Enumerates annotations from the collector as an [`AnnotationEnumerator`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaannotationproducer-annotationenumerator.html).
Body: Enumerates annotations from the collector as an [`AnnotationEnumerator`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonschemaannotationproducer-annotationenumerator.html).

Description: Writes all annotations from the collector as a JSON object to the given writer.
Body: Writes all annotations from the collector as a JSON object to the given writer.

Title: JsonSchemaAnnotationProducer.CollectAnnotations
Description: Collects all annotations from the collector into a dictionary keyed by (instanceLocation, keyword), with values being a dictionary of schemaLocation → annotationValue.
Body: Collects all annotations from the collector into a dictionary keyed by (instanceLocation, keyword), with values being a dictionary of schemaLocation → annotationValue.

Create: Creates an instance of a [`JsonWorkspace`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonworkspace.html).

Title: JsonWriterOptions
Description: Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when writing JSON using the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html). By default, the JSON is written without any indentation or extra white space. Also, the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) will throw an exception if the user attempts to write structurally invalid JSON.
Body:
Provides the ability for the user to define custom behavior when writing JSON using the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html). By default, the JSON is written without any indentation or extra white space. Also, the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) will throw an exception if the user attempts to write structurally invalid JSON.
Encoder: The encoder to use when escaping strings, or `null` to use the default encoder.
IndentCharacter: Defines the indentation character used by [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) when [`Indented`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonwriteroptions.indented.html) is enabled. Defaults to the space character.
Indented: Defines whether the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) should pretty print the JSON which includes: indenting nested JSON tokens, adding new lines, and adding white space between property names and values. By default, the JSON is written without any extra white space.
IndentSize: Defines the indentation size used by [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) when [`Indented`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonwriteroptions.indented.html) is enabled. Defaults to two.
MaxDepth: Gets or sets the maximum depth allowed when writing JSON, with the default (i.e. 0) indicating a max depth of 1000.
NewLine: Gets or sets the new line string to use when [`Indented`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonwriteroptions.indented.html) is `true`. The default is the value of [`NewLine`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.environment.newline).
SkipValidation: Defines whether the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) should skip structural validation and allow the user to write invalid JSON, when set to true. If set to false, any attempts to write invalid JSON will result in an [`InvalidOperationException`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.invalidoperationexception) to be thrown.

Title: ParsedJsonDocument<T>
Description: Represents the structure of a JSON value in a lightweight, read-only form.
Body:
Represents the structure of a JSON value in a lightweight, read-only form.
This class utilizes resources from pooled memory to minimize the garbage collector (GC) impact in high-usage scenarios. Failure to properly Dispose this object will result in the memory not being returned to the pool, which will cause an increase in GC impact across various parts of the framework.

ParseAsync: Parse a [`Stream`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.io.stream) as UTF-8 encoded data representing a single JSON value into a ParsedJsonDocument. The Stream will be read to completion.
Parse: Parses text representing a single JSON value into a ParsedJsonDocument.

ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader.

Title: Period
Description: Represents a period of time expressed in human chronological terms: hours, days, weeks, months and so on.
Body:
Represents a period of time expressed in human chronological terms: hours, days, weeks, months and so on.
A [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) contains a set of properties such as [`Years`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.years.html), [`Months`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.months.html), and so on that return the number of each unit contained within this period. Note that these properties are not normalized in any way by default, and so a [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) may contain values such as "2 hours and 90 minutes". The [`Normalize`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.normalize.html) method will convert equivalent periods into a standard representation. Periods can contain negative units as well as positive units ("+2 hours, -43 minutes, +10 seconds"), but do not differentiate between properties that are zero and those that are absent (i.e. a period created as "10 years" and one created as "10 years, zero months" are equal periods; the [`Months`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.months.html) property returns zero in both cases). [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) equality is implemented by comparing each property's values individually, without any normalization. (For example, a period of "24 hours" is not considered equal to a period of "1 day".) The static [`NormalizingEqualityComparer`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.normalizingequalitycomparer.html) comparer provides an equality comparer which performs normalization before comparisons. There is no natural ordering for periods, but [`CreateComparer`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.createcomparer.html) can be used to create a comparer which orders periods according to a reference date, by adding each period to that date and comparing the results. Periods operate on calendar-related types such as [`LocalDateTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.LocalDateTime.html) whereas [`Duration`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.Duration.html) operates on instants on the time line. (Note that although [`ZonedDateTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.ZonedDateTime.html) includes both concepts, it only supports duration-based arithmetic.) The complexity of each method in this type is hard to document precisely, and often depends on the calendar system involved in performing the actual calculations. Operations do not depend on the magnitude of the units in the period, other than for optimizations for values of zero or occasionally for particularly small values. For example, adding 10,000 days to a date does not require greater algorithmic complexity than adding 1,000 days to the same date.
MaxValue: A period containing the maximum value for all properties.
MinValue: A period containing the minimum value for all properties.
Zero: Gets a period containing only zero-valued properties.
NormalizingEqualityComparer: Gets an equality comparer which compares periods by first normalizing them - so 24 hours is deemed equal to 1 day, and so on. Note that as per the [`Normalize`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.normalize.html) method, years and months are unchanged by normalization - so 12 months does not equal 1 year.
Nanoseconds: Gets the number of nanoseconds within this period.
Ticks: Gets the number of ticks within this period.
Milliseconds: Gets the number of milliseconds within this period.
Seconds: Gets the number of seconds within this period.
Minutes: Gets the number of minutes within this period.
Hours: Gets the number of hours within this period.
Days: Gets the number of days within this period.
Weeks: Gets the number of weeks within this period.
Months: Gets the number of months within this period.
Years: Gets the number of years within this period.
HasTimeComponent: Gets a value indicating whether or not this period contains any non-zero-valued time-based properties (hours or lower).
HasDateComponent: Gets a value indicating whether or not this period contains any non-zero date-based properties (days or higher).
TryParse: Parses a string into a Period.
FromYears: Creates a period representing the specified number of years.
FromMonths: Creates a period representing the specified number of months.
FromWeeks: Creates a period representing the specified number of weeks.
FromDays: Creates a period representing the specified number of days.
FromHours: Creates a period representing the specified number of hours.
FromMinutes: Creates a period representing the specified number of minutes.
FromSeconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of seconds.
FromMilliseconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of milliseconds.
FromTicks: Creates a period representing the specified number of ticks.
FromNanoseconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of nanoseconds.
Add: Adds two periods together, by simply adding the values for each property.
CreateComparer: Creates an [`IComparer`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.generic.icomparer-1) for periods, using the given "base" local date/time.
Subtract: Subtracts one period from another, by simply subtracting each property value.
DaysBetween: Returns the number of days between two [`LocalDate`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.LocalDate.html) objects.
PeriodParser: A parser for a json period.
ToDuration: For periods that do not contain a non-zero number of years or months, returns a duration for this period assuming a standard 7-day week, 24-hour day, 60-minute hour etc.
Normalize: Returns a normalized version of this period, such that equivalent (but potentially non-equal) periods are changed to the same representation.
ToString: Returns this string formatted according to the ISO8601 duration specification used by JSON schema.
Equals: Compares the given object for equality with this one, as per [`Equals`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.equals.html). See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
GetHashCode: Returns the hash code for this period, consistent with [`Equals`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.equals.html). See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
Equals: Compares the given period for equality with this one. See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
Equals: Compares the given period for equality with this one. See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.

Title: PeriodBuilder
Description: A mutable builder class for [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) values. Each property can be set independently, and then a Period can be created from the result using the [`BuildPeriod`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-periodbuilder.buildperiod.html) method.
Body:
A mutable builder class for [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) values. Each property can be set independently, and then a Period can be created from the result using the [`BuildPeriod`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-periodbuilder.buildperiod.html) method.
Days: Gets or sets the number of days within the period.
Hours: Gets or sets the number of hours within the period.
Milliseconds: Gets or sets the number of milliseconds within the period.
Minutes: Gets or sets the number of minutes within the period.
Months: Gets or sets the number of months within the period.
Nanoseconds: Gets or sets the number of nanoseconds within the period.
Seconds: Gets or sets the number of seconds within the period.
Ticks: Gets or sets the number of ticks within the period.
Weeks: Gets or sets the number of weeks within the period.
Years: Gets or sets the number of years within the period.
this[PeriodUnits]: Gets or sets the value of a single unit.
BuildPeriod: Builds a period from the properties in this builder.

Title: RawUtf8JsonString
Description: Represents a raw UTF-8 JSON string.
Body:
Represents a raw UTF-8 JSON string.
This may use a rented buffer to back the string, so it is disposable.
Memory: Gets the underlying UTF-8 bytes as a [`ReadOnlyMemory`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.readonlymemory-1).
Span: Gets the underlying UTF-8 bytes as a [`ReadOnlySpan`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.readonlyspan-1).
TakeOwnership: Takes ownership of the underlying memory and any extra rented array pool bytes.
Dispose: Releases any rented array pool bytes and clears sensitive data.

Description: Gets the underlying UTF-8 bytes as a [`ReadOnlySpan`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.readonlyspan-1).
Body: Gets the underlying UTF-8 bytes as a [`ReadOnlySpan`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.readonlyspan-1).

Description: Takes ownership of the underlying memory and any extra rented array pool bytes.
Body: Takes ownership of the underlying memory and any extra rented array pool bytes.

Description: Releases any rented array pool bytes and clears sensitive data.
Body: Releases any rented array pool bytes and clears sensitive data.

Title: UnescapedUtf16JsonString
Description: Represents an Unescaped UTF-16 JSON string.
Body:
Represents an Unescaped UTF-16 JSON string.
This uses a rented buffer to back the string, so it is disposable.
Memory: Gets the UTF-16 characters as a read-only memory.
Span: Gets the UTF-16 characters as a read-only span.
TakeOwnership: Take ownership of the [`Shared`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.buffers.arraypool-1.shared) characters, if any.
Dispose: Disposes the unescaped UTF-16 JSON string, returning any rented array pool characters.

Title: Utf8Iri
Description: A UTF-8 IRI.
Body:
A UTF-8 IRI.
```csharp foo:// user@example.com:8042/over/there?name= ferret#nose \_/ \___________________/\_________/ \_________/ \__/ | | | | | scheme authority path query fragment \___/\______________/ | | user host (including port) ```
Authority: Gets the authority component of the reference.
Fragment: Gets the fragment component of the reference.
HasAuthority: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has an authority.
HasFragment: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a fragment.
HasHost: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a host.
HasPath: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a path.
HasPort: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a port.
HasQuery: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a query.
HasScheme: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a scheme.
HasUser: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a user.
Host: Gets the host component of the reference (includes both host and port).
IsDefaultPort: Gets a value indicating whether this is the default port for the scheme.
IsRelative: Gets a value indicating whether this is a relative IRI.
IsValid: Gets a value indicating whether this is a valid IRI.
OriginalIri: Gets the original (fully encoded) string.
Path: Gets the path component of the IRI.
Port: Gets the port component of the IRI as a byte span.
PortValue: Gets the port value as an integer.
Query: Gets the query component of the IRI.
Scheme: Gets the scheme component of the IRI.
User: Gets the user component of the IRI.
CreateIri: Creates a new UTF-8 IRI from the specified IRI bytes.
TryCreateIri: Tries to create a new UTF-8 IRI from the specified IRI bytes.
GetUri: Gets the value as a [`Uri`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uri).
TryFormatDisplay: Gets the IRI in canonical form for display.
TryFormatCanonical: Gets the IRI in canonical form.
TryApply: Applies the given IRI to the current (base) IRI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base IRI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryApply: Applies the given IRI reference to the current (base) IRI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base IRI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryApply: Applies the given URI reference to the current (base) IRI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base IRI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryApply: Applies the given URI reference to the current (base) IRI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base IRI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryMakeRelative: Makes a relative IRI reference from the current (base) IRI to the target IRI. If the scheme, host, and port match, a relative reference is created; otherwise, the full target IRI is returned.
TryMakeRelative: Makes a relative IRI reference from the current (base) IRI to the target URI. If the scheme, host, and port match, a relative reference is created; otherwise, the full target URI is returned.
ToString: Returns a string representation of the IRI in display format.

Title: Utf8IriReference.TryApply
Description: Applies the given IRI to the current (base) IRI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base IRI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed. This will fail if the IRI reference is a relative reference.

Title: Utf8IriReferenceValue
Description: A UTF-8 IRI reference value that has been parsed from a JSON document.
Body:
A UTF-8 IRI reference value that has been parsed from a JSON document.
This type should be used in a using declaration to ensure that the underlying memory is released when it is no longer needed.
IriReference: Gets the UTF-8 IRI reference value.
TryGetValue: Tries to get the value of the element at the specified index as a [`Utf8IriReferenceValue`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8irireferencevalue.html).
Dispose: Disposes the underlying resources used to store the UTF-8 string backing the IRI reference value.

Title: Utf8JsonPointer
Body:
IsValid: Gets a value indicating whether this is a valid IRI.
TryCreateJsonPointer: Tries to create a new UTF-8 JSON Pointer from the specified UTF-8 bytes.
TryResolve: Try to resolve the path specified by this JSON Pointer against the provided JSON element, returning the value at that path if it exists.
DecodeSegment: Decodes a single JSON Pointer segment, unescaping `~1` to `/` and `~0` to `~` as specified by RFC 6901.
TryGetLineAndOffset: Try to resolve the path specified by this JSON Pointer against the provided JSON element, returning the 1-based line number and character offset of the target element in the original source document.

Title: Utf8JsonReader
Description: Provides a high-performance API for forward-only, read-only access to the UTF-8 encoded JSON text. It processes the text sequentially with no caching and adheres strictly to the JSON RFC by default (https:// tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259). When it encounters invalid JSON, it throws a JsonException with basic error information like line number and byte position on the line. Since this type is a ref struct, it does not directly support async. However, it does provide support for reentrancy to read incomplete data, and continue reading once more data is presented. To be able to set max depth while reading OR allow skipping comments, create an instance of [`JsonReaderState`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonreaderstate.html) and pass that in to the reader.
Body:
Provides a high-performance API for forward-only, read-only access to the UTF-8 encoded JSON text. It processes the text sequentially with no caching and adheres strictly to the JSON RFC by default (https:// tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259). When it encounters invalid JSON, it throws a JsonException with basic error information like line number and byte position on the line. Since this type is a ref struct, it does not directly support async. However, it does provide support for reentrancy to read incomplete data, and continue reading once more data is presented. To be able to set max depth while reading OR allow skipping comments, create an instance of [`JsonReaderState`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonreaderstate.html) and pass that in to the reader.
ValueSpan: Gets the value of the last processed token as a ReadOnlySpan<byte> slice of the input payload. If the JSON is provided within a ReadOnlySequence<byte> and the slice that represents the token value fits in a single segment, then [`ValueSpan`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.valuespan.html) will contain the sliced value since it can be represented as a span. Otherwise, the [`ValueSequence`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.valuesequence.html) will contain the token value.
BytesConsumed: Returns the total amount of bytes consumed by the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) so far for the current instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) with the given UTF-8 encoded input text.
TokenStartIndex: Returns the index that the last processed JSON token starts at within the given UTF-8 encoded input text, skipping any white space.
CurrentDepth: Tracks the recursive depth of the nested objects / arrays within the JSON text processed so far. This provides the depth of the current token.
TokenType: Gets the type of the last processed JSON token in the UTF-8 encoded JSON text.
HasValueSequence: Lets the caller know which of the two 'Value' properties to read to get the token value. For input data within a ReadOnlySpan<byte> this will always return false. For input data within a ReadOnlySequence<byte>, this will only return true if the token value straddles more than a single segment and hence couldn't be represented as a span.
ValueIsEscaped: Lets the caller know whether the current [`ValueSpan`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.valuespan.html) or [`ValueSequence`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.valuesequence.html) properties contain escape sequences per RFC 8259 section 7, and therefore require unescaping before being consumed.
IsFinalBlock: Returns the mode of this instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html). True when the reader was constructed with the input span containing the entire data to process. False when the reader was constructed knowing that the input span may contain partial data with more data to follow.
Position: Returns the current [`SequencePosition`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sequenceposition) within the provided UTF-8 encoded input ReadOnlySequence<byte>. If the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) was constructed with a ReadOnlySpan<byte> instead, this will always return a default [`SequencePosition`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sequenceposition).
CurrentState: Returns the current snapshot of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) state which must be captured by the caller and passed back in to the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) ctor with more data. Unlike the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html), which is a ref struct, the state can survive across async/await boundaries and hence this type is required to provide support for reading in more data asynchronously before continuing with a new instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html).
Read: Read the next JSON token from input source.
Skip: Skips the children of the current JSON token.
TrySkip: Tries to skip the children of the current JSON token.
ValueTextEquals: Compares the UTF-8 encoded text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match.
ValueTextEquals: Compares the string text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match.
ValueTextEquals: Compares the text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match.
CopyString: Copies the current JSON token value from the source, unescaped as a UTF-8 string to the destination buffer.
CopyString: Copies the current JSON token value from the source, unescaped, and transcoded as a UTF-16 char buffer.
GetBoolean: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Boolean`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.boolean). Returns `true` if the TokenType is JsonTokenType.True and `false` if the TokenType is JsonTokenType.False.
GetByte: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Byte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.byte). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Byte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.byte) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetBytesFromBase64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source and decodes the Base64 encoded JSON string as bytes.
GetComment: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a comment, transcoded as a [`String`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.string).
GetDateTime: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetDateTimeOffset: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`DateTimeOffset`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetimeoffset). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`DateTimeOffset`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetimeoffset) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetDecimal: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetDouble: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Double`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.double). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Double`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.double) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetGuid: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetInt16: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Int16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int16). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Int16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int16) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetInt32: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as an [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to an [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetInt64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetSByte: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as an [`SByte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sbyte). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to an [`SByte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sbyte) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetSingle: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Single`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Single`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetString: Parses the current JSON token value from the source, unescaped, and transcoded as a [`String`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.string).
GetUInt16: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint16). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint16) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetUInt32: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint32). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint32) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
GetUInt64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint64). Returns the value if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint64) value. Throws exceptions otherwise.
TryGetByte: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Byte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.byte). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Byte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.byte) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetBytesFromBase64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source and decodes the Base64 encoded JSON string as bytes. Returns `true` if the entire token value is encoded as valid Base64 text and can be successfully decoded to bytes. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetDateTime: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetDateTimeOffset: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`DateTimeOffset`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetimeoffset). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`DateTimeOffset`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetimeoffset) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetDecimal: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetDouble: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Double`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.double). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Double`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.double) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetGuid: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid) value. Only supports [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid) values with hyphens and without any surrounding decorations. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetInt16: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Int16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int16). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Int16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int16) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetInt32: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as an [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to an [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetInt64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetSByte: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as an [`SByte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sbyte). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to an [`SByte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sbyte) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetSingle: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`Single`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`Single`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetUInt16: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint16). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint16) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetUInt32: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint32). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint32) value. Returns `false` otherwise.
TryGetUInt64: Parses the current JSON token value from the source as a [`UInt64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint64). Returns `true` if the entire UTF-8 encoded token value can be successfully parsed to a [`UInt64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint64) value. Returns `false` otherwise.

Title: Utf8JsonReader.CurrentState
Description: Returns the current snapshot of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) state which must be captured by the caller and passed back in to the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) ctor with more data. Unlike the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html), which is a ref struct, the state can survive across async/await boundaries and hence this type is required to provide support for reading in more data asynchronously before continuing with a new instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html).
Body: Returns the current snapshot of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) state which must be captured by the caller and passed back in to the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) ctor with more data. Unlike the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html), which is a ref struct, the state can survive across async/await boundaries and hence this type is required to provide support for reading in more data asynchronously before continuing with a new instance of the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html).

Title: Utf8JsonReader.ValueTextEquals
Description: Compares the UTF-8 encoded text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match.
Body: Compares the UTF-8 encoded text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match. Compares the string text to the unescaped JSON token value in the source and returns true if they match. Title: Utf8JsonReader.CopyString

Body: Copies the current JSON token value from the source, unescaped as a UTF-8 string to the destination buffer. Copies the current JSON token value from the source, unescaped, and transcoded as a UTF-16 char buffer.

Title: Utf8JsonWriter
Description: Provides a high-performance API for forward-only, non-cached writing of UTF-8 encoded JSON text.
Body:
Provides a high-performance API for forward-only, non-cached writing of UTF-8 encoded JSON text.
It writes the text sequentially with no caching and adheres to the JSON RFC by default (https:// tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259), with the exception of writing comments. When the user attempts to write invalid JSON and validation is enabled, it throws an [`InvalidOperationException`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.invalidoperationexception) with a context specific error message. To be able to format the output with indentation and whitespace OR to skip validation, create an instance of [`JsonWriterOptions`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonwriteroptions.html) and pass that in to the writer.
BytesPending: Returns the amount of bytes written by the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) so far that have not yet been flushed to the output and committed.
BytesCommitted: Returns the amount of bytes committed to the output by the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) so far.
Options: Gets the custom behavior when writing JSON using the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) which indicates whether to format the output while writing and whether to skip structural JSON validation or not.
CurrentDepth: Tracks the recursive depth of the nested objects / arrays within the JSON text written so far. This provides the depth of the current token.
WriteBase64StringSegment: Writes the input bytes as a partial JSON string.
WriteStringValueSegment: Writes the text value segment as a partial JSON string.
WriteStringValueSegment: Writes the UTF-8 text value segment as a partial JSON string.
Reset: Resets the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) internal state so that it can be re-used.
Reset: Resets the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) internal state so that it can be re-used with the new instance of [`Stream`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.io.stream).
Reset: Resets the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) internal state so that it can be re-used with the new instance of [`IBufferWriter`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.buffers.ibufferwriter-1).
Flush: Commits the JSON text written so far which makes it visible to the output destination.
Dispose: Commits any left over JSON text that has not yet been flushed and releases all resources used by the current instance.
DisposeAsync: Asynchronously commits any left over JSON text that has not yet been flushed and releases all resources used by the current instance.
FlushAsync: Asynchronously commits the JSON text written so far which makes it visible to the output destination.
WriteStartArray: Writes the beginning of a JSON array.
WriteStartObject: Writes the beginning of a JSON object.
WriteStartArray: Writes the beginning of a JSON array with a pre-encoded property name as the key.
WriteStartObject: Writes the beginning of a JSON object with a pre-encoded property name as the key.
WriteStartArray: Writes the beginning of a JSON array with a property name as the key.
WriteStartObject: Writes the beginning of a JSON object with a property name as the key.
WriteStartArray: Writes the beginning of a JSON array with a property name as the key.
WriteStartObject: Writes the beginning of a JSON object with a property name as the key.
WriteStartArray: Writes the beginning of a JSON array with a property name as the key.
WriteStartObject: Writes the beginning of a JSON object with a property name as the key.
WriteEndArray: Writes the end of a JSON array.
WriteEndObject: Writes the end of a JSON object.
WriteBase64String: Writes the pre-encoded property name and raw bytes value (as a Base64 encoded JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteBase64String: Writes the property name and raw bytes value (as a Base64 encoded JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteBase64String: Writes the property name and raw bytes value (as a Base64 encoded JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteBase64String: Writes the property name and raw bytes value (as a Base64 encoded JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteString: Writes the pre-encoded property name and [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value (as a JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteString: Writes the property name and [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value (as a JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteString: Writes the property name and [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value (as a JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.
WriteString: Writes the property name and [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value (as a JSON string) as part of a name/value pair of a JSON object.

Title: Utf8JsonWriter.BytesPending

Title: Utf8JsonWriter.BytesCommitted

Body: Returns the amount of bytes committed to the output by the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) so far.

Title: Utf8JsonWriter.Options
Description: Gets the custom behavior when writing JSON using the [`Utf8JsonWriter`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonwriter.html) which indicates whether to format the output while writing and whether to skip structural JSON validation or not.

Title: Utf8JsonWriter.CurrentDepth

Title: Utf8Uri
Description: A UTF-8 URI.
Body:
A UTF-8 URI.
```csharp foo:// user@example.com:8042/over/there?name= ferret#nose \_/ \___________________/\_________/ \_________/ \__/ | | | | | scheme authority path query fragment \___/\______________/ | | user host (including port) ```
Authority: Gets the authority component of the reference.
Fragment: Gets the fragment component of the reference.
HasAuthority: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has an authority.
HasFragment: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a fragment.
HasHost: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a host.
HasPath: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a path.
HasPort: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a port.
HasQuery: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a query.
HasScheme: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a scheme.
HasUser: Gets a value indicating whether this reference has a user.
Host: Gets the host component of the reference (includes both host and port).
IsDefaultPort: Gets a value indicating whether this is the default port for the scheme.
IsRelative: Gets a value indicating whether this is a relative URI.
IsValid: Gets a value indicating whether this is a valid URI.
OriginalUri: Gets the original (fully encoded) string.
Path: Gets the path component of the URI.
Port: Gets the port component of the URI as a byte span.
PortValue: Gets the port value as an integer.
Query: Gets the query component of the URI.
Scheme: Gets the scheme component of the URI.
User: Gets the user component of the URI.
CreateUri: Creates a new UTF-8 URI from the specified URI bytes.
TryCreateUri: Tries to create a new UTF-8 URI from the specified URI bytes.
GetUri: Gets the value as a [`Uri`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uri).
TryFormatDisplay: Gets the URI in canonical form for display.
TryFormatCanonical: Gets the URI in canonical form.
TryApply: Applies the given URI reference to the current (base) URI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base URI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryApply: Applies the given URI reference to the current (base) URI and writes the result to the provided buffer. It uses the rules of RFC 3986 Section 5.2 to resolve the reference against the base URI, including handling of relative references and merging of paths as needed.
TryMakeRelative: Makes a relative URI reference from the current (base) URI to the target URI. If the scheme, host, and port match, a relative reference is created; otherwise, the full target URI is returned.
ToString: Returns a string representation of the URI in display format.
TryEscapeDataString: Escapes a UTF-8 string per RFC 3986 `EscapeDataString` semantics. Unreserved characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, '-', '_', '.', '~') pass through; all other bytes are percent-encoded as `%XX` with uppercase hex digits.
TryUnescapeDataString: Unescapes a percent-encoded UTF-8 string, matching `Uri.UnescapeDataString` semantics. Valid `%XX` sequences are decoded. Non-ASCII decoded bytes are only emitted when consecutive `%XX` sequences form a valid UTF-8 multi-byte sequence; invalid multi-byte sequences remain in their encoded form. Malformed sequences (invalid hex digits, truncated `%` at end of input) are copied through literally.
TryEscapeUri: Escapes a UTF-8 string with URI semantics (matching JavaScript's `encodeURI`). RFC 3986 unreserved characters plus reserved characters (`: / ? # [ ] @ ! $ & ' ( ) * + , ; =`) pass through unescaped; all other bytes are percent-encoded as `%XX` with uppercase hex digits.

Title: ComplexValueBuilder
Description: Provides a high-performance, low-allocation builder for constructing complex JSON values (objects and arrays) within an [`IMutableJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-imutablejsondocument.html).
Body:
Provides a high-performance, low-allocation builder for constructing complex JSON values (objects and arrays) within an [`IMutableJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-imutablejsondocument.html).
[`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html) is a ref struct designed for use in stack-based scenarios, enabling efficient construction of JSON objects and arrays by directly manipulating the underlying metadata database. This builder supports adding properties and items of various types, including primitives, strings, numbers, booleans, nulls, and complex/nested values. It also provides methods for starting and ending JSON objects and arrays, as well as for integrating with [`IMutableJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-imutablejsondocument.html) for document mutation. Typical usage involves creating a builder via [`Create`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.create.html), using [`AddProperty`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.addproperty.html) and [`AddItem`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.additem.html) methods to populate the structure, and then finalizing with [`EndObject`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.endobject.html) or [`EndArray`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.endarray.html). This type is not thread-safe and must not be stored on the heap.
MemberCount: Gets the number of members (properties or items) added to the current object or array.
Create: Creates a new [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html) for the specified parent document, pre-allocating space for the given number of elements.

Title: EnumStringMap
Description: A hash map from UTF-8 string keys to integer values for efficient O(1) average-case lookup.
Body:
A hash map from UTF-8 string keys to integer values for efficient O(1) average-case lookup.
This type is used by generated code for oneOf discriminator dispatch when the number of branches exceeds a threshold. It uses the same hash algorithm as [`EnumStringSet`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-enumstringset.html) and [`PropertySchemaMatchers`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-propertyschemamatchers-t.html).
TryGetValue: Attempts to find the integer value associated with the specified UTF-8 string key.

Title: EnumStringMap.TryGetValue
Description: Attempts to find the integer value associated with the specified UTF-8 string key.
Body: Attempts to find the integer value associated with the specified UTF-8 string key.

Title: EnumStringSet
Description: A hash set of UTF-8 string values for efficient O(1) average-case membership testing.
Body:
A hash set of UTF-8 string values for efficient O(1) average-case membership testing.
This type is used by generated code for enum validation when the number of string enum values exceeds a threshold. It uses the same hash algorithm as [`PropertySchemaMatchers`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-propertyschemamatchers-t.html) (custom hash function, separate chaining, ArrayPool-backed storage) but is optimized for the set-membership use case — no associated values, just [`Contains`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-enumstringset.contains.html).
Contains: Tests whether the specified UTF-8 string value is a member of this set.

Title: EnumStringSet.Contains
Description: Tests whether the specified UTF-8 string value is a member of this set.
Body: Tests whether the specified UTF-8 string value is a member of this set.

Title: FixedJsonValueDocument<T>
Description: Represents a JSON document backed by a fixed simple value (number or string).
Body:
Represents a JSON document backed by a fixed simple value (number or string).
This type uses a thread-local pool to avoid allocations for intermediate values produced during expression evaluation (e.g., arithmetic results, string concatenation results). Each instance wraps the raw UTF-8 JSON representation of a single value. For numbers, the raw value is the unquoted number text (e.g., `123.45`). For strings, the raw value is the quoted string text (e.g., `"hello"`).
RootElement: Gets the root element of the document.
ForNumber: Creates a [`FixedJsonValueDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-fixedjsonvaluedocument-t.html) for a number value, using the pool.
ForString: Creates a [`FixedJsonValueDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-fixedjsonvaluedocument-t.html) for a string value, using the pool.
ForNumberFromSpan: Creates a [`FixedJsonValueDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-fixedjsonvaluedocument-t.html) for a number value from a span, copying the data into the document's internal buffer (zero heap allocation).
ForStringFromSpan: Creates a [`FixedJsonValueDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-fixedjsonvaluedocument-t.html) for a string value from a span, copying the data into the document's internal buffer (zero heap allocation).

Title: FixedJsonValueDocument<T>.RootElement
Description: Gets the root element of the document.
Body: Gets the root element of the document.

Title: FixedStringJsonDocument<T>
Description: Represents a JSON document based on a fixed string value.
Body:
Represents a JSON document based on a fixed string value.
This type uses an internal cache to avoid allocations for evaluatoin of string values that have not originated in a regular JSON document (e.g. property names, or external strings.)
Parse: Parse an instance of the fixed string to a document, using caching.

Title: FixedStringJsonDocument<T>.Parse
Description: Parse an instance of the fixed string to a document, using caching.
Body: Parse an instance of the fixed string to a document, using caching.

Title: IJsonDocument.WriteElementToMetadataDb
Description: Writes the element at the given index into the specified metadata database at a specific position, creating external reference rows. This is the positional variant of [`AppendElementToMetadataDb`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-ijsondocument.appendelementtometadatadb.html).

Title: IJsonDocument.TryGetLineAndOffset

Title: IJsonDocument.TryGetLineAndOffsetForPointer

Title: IJsonDocument.TryGetLine

Body: Tries to get the specified line from the original source document as UTF-8 bytes. Tries to get the specified line from the original source document as a string.

Title: IMutableJsonDocument
Description: Represents a mutable JSON document that supports editing and value storage operations.
Body:
Represents a mutable JSON document that supports editing and value storage operations.
Version: Gets the version of the document.
ParentWorkspaceIndex: Gets the index of the parent workspace.
Workspace: Gets the JSON workspace associated with this document.
FreezeElement: Creates a frozen (immutable) copy of the element at the specified index, backed by a new document builder registered in the same workspace.
GetArrayIndexElement: Gets the array element at the specified index as a mutable JSON element.
GetArrayIndexElement: Gets the element at the specified array index within the current index.
TryGetNamedPropertyValueIndex: Gets the named property value from a specific [`MetadataDb`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-metadatadb.html).

StoreRawNumberValue: Stores a raw number value in the document.
StoreNullValue: Stores a null value in the document.
StoreBooleanValue: Stores a boolean value in the document.
EscapeAndStoreRawStringValue: Escapes and stores a raw string value in the document.
EscapeAndStoreRawStringValue: Escapes and stores a raw string value in the document.
StoreRawStringValue: Stores a raw string value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Guid`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.guid) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`DateTime`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`DateTimeOffset`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetimeoffset) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`OffsetDateTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.OffsetDateTime.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`OffsetDate`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.OffsetDate.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`OffsetTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.OffsetTime.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`LocalDate`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.LocalDate.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Period`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-period.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`SByte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.sbyte) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Byte`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.byte) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`Int32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`UInt32`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint32) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`UInt64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint64) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Int16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int16) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`UInt16`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint16) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Single`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Double`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.double) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Decimal`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.decimal) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`BigInteger`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.numerics.biginteger) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`BigNumber`](/api/v5/corvus-numerics-bignumber.html) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores an [`Int128`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int128) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`UInt128`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.uint128) value in the document.
StoreValue: Stores a [`Half`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.half) value in the document.
RemoveRange: Removes a range of values from the document.
SetAndDispose: Sets the value of the document and disposes the provided [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
ReplaceRootAndDispose: Replaces the root value of an already-initialized document, disposing the old value and the provided [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
InsertAndDispose: Inserts a value into the document and disposes the provided [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
OverwriteAndDispose: Overwrites values in the document and disposes the provided [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
InsertSimpleValue: Inserts a single simple value row directly into a complex object, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
OverwriteSimpleValue: Overwrites a range with a single simple value row directly, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
InsertSimpleProperty: Inserts a property name and a single simple value row directly, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
InsertFromDocument: Inserts element rows from a source document directly, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
OverwriteFromDocument: Overwrites a range with element rows from a source document, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
InsertPropertyFromDocument: Inserts a property name row followed by element rows from a source document, bypassing [`ComplexValueBuilder`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-complexvaluebuilder.html).
TryReplacePropertyValue: Replaces an existing property value with a simple scalar value.
TryReplacePropertyFromDocument: Replaces an existing property value with element rows from a source document.
CopyValueToProperty: Copies a value and sets it as a property on a destination object. If the property already exists, it is replaced.
CopyValueToArrayIndex: Copies a value and inserts it as an array item at the specified index.
CopyValueToArrayEnd: Copies a value and appends it at the end of a destination array.
MovePropertyToProperty: Moves a property from a source object to a destination object as a new property. Handles removing existing destination properties and same-property no-ops.
MovePropertyToArray: Moves a property from a source object into a destination array at the specified index.
MovePropertyToArrayEnd: Moves a property from a source object to the end of a destination array.
MoveItemToArray: Moves an array item from a source array into a destination array at the specified index. Handles same-array moves with post-removal index semantics.
MoveItemToArrayEnd: Moves an array item from a source array to the end of a destination array.
MoveItemToProperty: Moves an array item from a source array to a destination object as a new property. Handles removing existing destination properties.

Title: IMutableJsonElement<T>
Description: Represents a mutable JSON element of type `T`.
Body:
Represents a mutable JSON element of type `T`.
Note that mutable elements are ephemeral. If their underlying document is modified, they may no longer be valid, and their behaviour is undefined.

Title: IWorkspaceManagedDocument
Description: Marker interface for documents whose lifecycle is managed by a [`JsonWorkspace`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonworkspace.html). When a workspace is disposed or reset, all registered documents implementing this interface are disposed, returning pooled resources.
Body:
Marker interface for documents whose lifecycle is managed by a [`JsonWorkspace`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonworkspace.html). When a workspace is disposed or reset, all registered documents implementing this interface are disposed, returning pooled resources.
Implemented by [`IMutableJsonDocument`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-imutablejsondocument.html) (builders) and by pooled value documents such as `FixedJsonValueDocument`. Not implemented by caller-owned immutable documents like `ParsedJsonDocument`.

Title: JsonDocument
Description: Base class for JSON document implementations providing common functionality for parsing and accessing JSON data.
Body:
Base class for JSON document implementations providing common functionality for parsing and accessing JSON data.

Freeze: Makes the document immutable.
