Title: JsonElementHelpers
Description: Core helper methods for parsing and processing JSON numeric values into their component parts.
Body:
Core helper methods for parsing and processing JSON numeric values into their component parts.
ParseNumber: Parses a JSON number into its component parts using normal-form decimal representation.
TryParseNumber: Parses a JSON number into its component parts using normal-form decimal representation.
CompareNormalizedJsonNumbers: Compares two normalized JSON numbers for equality.
ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided span.
ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided span.
ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided text.
ParseValue: Parses one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader.
TryParseValue: Attempts to parse one JSON value (including objects or arrays) from the provided reader.
CountRunes: Count the runes in a UTF-8 string.
ParseDateCore: Parses a date string in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) and extracts the year, month, and day components.
ParseOffsetTimeCore: Parses a time string with optional offset in ISO 8601 format and extracts the time and offset components.
ParseOffsetCore: Parses a timezone offset string in ISO 8601 format (±HH:MM or Z) and extracts the offset in seconds.
ParseTimeCore: Parses a time string in ISO 8601 format (HH:MM:SS[.nnnnnnnnn]) and extracts the time components.
SetPropertyUnsafe: Sets a property value on a target element.
RemovePropertyUnsafe: Removes a property value from a target element.
RemovePropertyUnsafe: Removes a property value from a target element.
RemoveRangeUnsafe: Removes a range of items from an array element.
RemoveFirstUnsafe: Removes the first array element that equals the specified item.
RemoveWhereUnsafe: Removes a items from an array element which match a predicate.
ApplyUnsafe: Applies all properties from a source JSON object element to a target JSON object element.
ToValueKind: Converts a [`JsonTokenType`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-jsontokentype.html) to its corresponding [`JsonValueKind`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-jsonvaluekind.html).
GetUtf8StringLength: Gets the length of a UTF-8 encoded string in characters (not bytes).
GetParentDocumentAndIndex: Gets the parent document and document index for a JSON element.
AreEqualJsonNumbers: Compares two valid UTF-8 encoded JSON numbers for decimal equality.
AreEqualNormalizedJsonNumbers: Compares two valid normalized JSON numbers for decimal equality.
IsIntegerNormalizedJsonNumber: Determines if a JSON number is an integer.
IsMultipleOf: Determines whether the normalized JSON number is an exact multiple of the given integer divisor.
IsMultipleOf: Determines whether the normalized JSON number is an exact multiple of the given integer divisor.
TryFormatNumberAsString: Format the number as a string.
TryGetNormalizedInt64: Attempts to convert a normalized JSON number to a [`Int64`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int64) value.
TryFormatUri: Tries to format a [`Utf8Uri`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8uri.html) as a display (human-readable) or canonical (percent-encoded) string.
DeepEquals: Compares the values of two [`IJsonElement`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-ijsonelement.html) values for equality, including the values of all descendant elements.
DeepEqualsNoParentDocumentCheck: Compares the values of two [`IJsonElement`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-internal-ijsonelement.html) values for equality, including the values of all descendant elements.
DeepEqualsNoParentDocumentCheck: Compares the values of two JSON values for equality, including the values of all descendant elements.
TryFormatLocalDate: Format a date as a UTF-8 string.
TryFormatOffsetDate: Format a date as a UTF-8 string.
TryFormatOffsetDateTime: Format an offset date time as a UTF-8 string.
TryFormatOffsetTime: Format a time as a UTF-8 string.
TryFormatPeriod: Format a period as a UTF-8 string for the `duration` format.
ParsePeriod: Parse a period from a UTF-8 encoded string for the `duration` format.

Title: JsonSchemaContext
Description: The context for a JSON schema evaluation.
Body:
The context for a JSON schema evaluation.
IsMatch: Gets a value indicating whether the context represents a match.
HasCollector: Gets a value indicating whether this context has a [`IJsonSchemaResultsCollector`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-ijsonschemaresultscollector.html).
RequiresEvaluationTracking: Gets a value indicating whether this context requires evaluation tracking.
BeginContext: Begins a new JSON schema evaluation context for the specified document.
PushChildContextUnescaped: Creates a new child context for schema evaluation with unescaped property name tracking.
PushChildContext: Creates a new child context for schema evaluation with typed provider context for path generation.
PushChildContext: Creates a new child context for schema evaluation with optional path providers.
CommitChildContext: Commits a child context back to its parent, merging validation results and cleaning up resources.
CommitChildContext: Commits a child context back to its parent, merging validation results and cleaning up resources.
EndContext: Ends the root evaluation context, committing any pending results to the results collector.
EvaluatedBooleanSchema: Records the evaluation of a boolean schema.
EvaluatedKeyword: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword.
EvaluatedKeyword: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword with a provider context.
EvaluatedKeywordForProperty: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword for a specific property.
EvaluatedKeywordForProperty: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword for a specific property with a provider context.
EvaluatedKeywordPath: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword using a path-based approach.
EvaluatedKeywordPath: Records the evaluation of a schema keyword using a path-based approach with a provider context.
IgnoredKeyword: Records that a keyword was ignored during schema evaluation.
IgnoredKeyword: Records that a keyword was ignored during schema evaluation with a provider context.
PopChildContext: Pops the most recently pushed child context without committing changes.
HasLocalEvaluatedItem: Determines whether a specific item at the given index has been locally evaluated.
HasLocalEvaluatedProperty: Determines whether a specific property at the given index has been locally evaluated.
HasLocalOrAppliedEvaluatedItem: Determines whether a specific item at the given index has been either locally or applied evaluated.
HasLocalOrAppliedEvaluatedProperty: Determines whether a specific property at the given index has been either locally or applied evaluated.
ApplyEvaluated: Applies the evaluated properties/items from the child context to this (parent) context, if appropriate.
AddLocalEvaluatedItem: Adds an item at the specified index to the local evaluated items collection.
AddLocalEvaluatedProperty: Adds a property at the specified index to the local evaluated properties collection.
AddAppliedEvaluatedProperty: Adds a property at the specified index to the applied evaluated properties collection.

Title: JsonSchemaEvaluation
Description: Support for JSON Schema matching implementations.
Body:
Support for JSON Schema matching implementations.
MatchTypeNull: Matches a JSON token type against the "null" type constraint.
MatchTypeBoolean: Matches a JSON token type against the "boolean" type constraint.
MatchRegularExpression: Validates that a string length equals the given value.
MatchRegularExpression: Validates that a string length equals the given value.
MatchNoopRegularExpression: Records a successful match for a noop regular expression pattern (one that always matches, such as `.*`).
MatchNonEmptyRegularExpression: Validates that a string is non-empty, as a replacement for patterns like `.+`.
MatchPrefixRegularExpression: Validates that a string starts with a literal prefix, as a replacement for patterns like `^prefix`.
MatchRangeRegularExpression: Validates that a string's Unicode codepoint length is within a range, as a replacement for patterns like `^.{n,m}$`.
MatchRangeRegularExpression: Validates that a UTF-8 byte span's Unicode codepoint length is within a range. Used for `patternProperties` matching where no context reporting is needed.
MatchStringConstantValue: Validates that a string equals the given value.
MatchLengthEquals: Validates that a string length equals the given value.
MatchLengthNotEquals: Validates that a string length does not equal the given value.
MatchLengthGreaterThan: Validates that a string length is greater than the given value.
MatchLengthGreaterThanOrEquals: Validates that a string length is greater than or equal to the given value.
MatchLengthLessThan: Validates that a string length is less than the given value.
MatchLengthLessThanOrEquals: Validates that a string length is less than or equal to the given value.
MatchDate: Validates that a string value conforms to the ISO 8601 date format.
MatchDateTime: Validates that a string value conforms to the ISO 8601 offset date-time format.
MatchDuration: Validates that a string value conforms to the ISO 8601 duration format.
MatchEmail: Validates that a string value is a valid email address format.
MatchHostname: Validates that a string value is a valid hostname format.
MatchIdnEmail: Validates that a string value is a valid internationalized domain name (IDN) email address format.
MatchIdnHostname: Validates that a string value is a valid internationalized domain name (IDN) hostname format.
MatchIPV4: Validates that a string value is a valid IPv4 address format.
MatchIPV6: Validates that a string value is a valid IPv6 address format.
MatchIri: Validates that a string value is a valid Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI) format.
MatchIriReference: Validates that a string value is a valid Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI) reference format.
MatchJsonPointer: Validates that a string value is a valid JSON Pointer format.
MatchRegex: Validates that a string value is a valid ECMAScript regular expression format.
MatchRelativeJsonPointer: Validates that a string value is a valid relative JSON Pointer format.
MatchTime: Validates that a string value conforms to the ISO 8601 offset time format.
MatchTypeString: Matches a JSON token type against the string type constraint.
MatchUri: Validates that a string value is a valid URI format.
MatchUriReference: Validates that a string value is a valid URI reference format (absolute or relative URI).
MatchUriTemplate: Validates that a string value is a valid URI template format.
MatchUuid: Validates that a string value is a valid UUID format.
MatchBase64String: Validates that a string value is a valid Base64-encoded string.
MatchJsonContent: Validates that a string value contains valid JSON content.
MatchBase64Content: Validates that a string value is a valid Base64-encoded JSON document.
SchemaLocationForIndexedKeyword: Creates a schema location for an indexed keyword by appending the index to the base location.
SchemaLocationForIndexedKeywordWithDependency: Creates a schema location for an indexed keyword by appending the index to the base location and dependency.
TryCopyMessage: Tries to copy a message to the specified buffer.
TryCopyPath: Tries to copy the path to the output buffer.
ExpectedType: Tries to write a message indicating the expected type for a value.
ExpectedMultipleOfDivisor: Tries to write a message indicating the expected type for a value.
IgnoredUnrecognizedFormat: Tries to write a message indicating that the format was not recognized.
IgnoredFormatNotAsserted: Tries to write a message indicating that the format was not asserted.
MatchTypeArray: Matches a JSON token type against the "array" type constraint.
SchemaLocationForItemIndex: Writes the schema location for an item at a specific index in an array.
ExpectedItemCountEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
ExpectedItemCountNotEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
ExpectedItemCountGreaterThanValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
ExpectedItemCountGreaterThanOrEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
ExpectedItemCountLessThanValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
ExpectedItemCountLessThanOrEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a item count.
MatchItemCountEquals: Validates that a item count equals the given value.
MatchItemCountNotEquals: Validates that a item count does not equal the given value.
MatchItemCountGreaterThan: Validates that a item count is greater than the given value.
MatchItemCountGreaterThanOrEquals: Validates that a item count is greater than or equal to the given value.
MatchItemCountLessThan: Validates that a item count is less than the given value.
MatchItemCountLessThanOrEquals: Validates that a item count is less than or equal to the given value.
ExpectedContainsCountEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
ExpectedContainsCountNotEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
ExpectedContainsCountGreaterThanValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
ExpectedContainsCountGreaterThanOrEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
ExpectedContainsCountLessThanValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
ExpectedContainsCountLessThanOrEqualsValue: Tries to write a message indicating the expected value for a contains count.
MatchContainsCountEquals: Validates that a contains count equals the given value.
MatchContainsCountNotEquals: Validates that a contains count does not equal the given value.
MatchContainsCountGreaterThan: Validates that a contains count is greater than the given value.
MatchContainsCountGreaterThanOrEquals: Validates that a contains count is greater than or equal to the given value.
MatchContainsCountLessThan: Validates that a contains count is less than the given value.
MatchContainsCountLessThanOrEquals: Validates that a contains count is less than or equal to the given value.
MatchTypeObject: Matches a JSON token type against the "object" type constraint.
MatchPropertyCountEquals: Validates that a property count equals the given value.

MatchPropertyCountGreaterThan: Validates that a property count is greater than the given value.

Title: JsonTokenType
Description: This enum defines the various JSON tokens that make up a JSON text and is used by the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) when moving from one token to the next. The [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) starts at 'None' by default. The 'Comment' enum value is only ever reached in a specific [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) mode and is not reachable by default.
Body: This enum defines the various JSON tokens that make up a JSON text and is used by the [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) when moving from one token to the next. The [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) starts at 'None' by default. The 'Comment' enum value is only ever reached in a specific [`Utf8JsonReader`](/api/v5/corvus-text-json-utf8jsonreader.html) mode and is not reachable by default.

Title: NormalizedJsonNumber
Description: Represents a normalized JSON number.
Body:
Represents a normalized JSON number.
IsNegative: Indicates whether the number is negative.
Integral: The normalized integral part of the original JSON representation of the number.
Fractional: The normalized fractional part of the original JSON representation of the number.
Exponent: The exponent to apply after concatenating the integral and fractional parts.

Description: Indicates whether the number is negative.

Title: NormalizedJsonNumber.Exponent

Title: PropertySchemaMatchers<T>
Description: A dictionary lookup of matchers for properties in a JSON object, optimized for low allocations and high performance.
Body:
A dictionary lookup of matchers for properties in a JSON object, optimized for low allocations and high performance.
This class uses a hash-based approach to enable O(1) average-case lookups of property matchers based on property names, while minimizing memory usage through array pooling and efficient data layout. The implementation includes a custom hash function, separate chaining for collision resolution, and optimized key comparison strategies to ensure fast lookups even in the presence of hash collisions.
TryGetNamedMatcher: Attempts to find the matcher for the named property value in the property map using efficient hash-based lookup.

Title: PropertySchemaMatchers<T>.TryGetNamedMatcher
Description: Attempts to find the matcher for the named property value in the property map using efficient hash-based lookup.
Body: Attempts to find the matcher for the named property value in the property map using efficient hash-based lookup.

Title: Utf8Hash
Description: Provides a fast hash function for short UTF-8 byte spans.
Body:
Provides a fast hash function for short UTF-8 byte spans.
For keys of 7 bytes or fewer, the hash encodes the entire key value, making it a perfect hash (no collisions possible among keys of that length). For longer keys, the hash mixes the first 8 bytes with the length and last byte.
GetHashCode: Calculates a hash code for the specified UTF-8 byte span.

Description: Calculates a hash code for the specified UTF-8 byte span.
Body: Calculates a hash code for the specified UTF-8 byte span.
